citations.quote: preoccupied with their thoughtsandimaginary constructs. They be comedetached, yethigh-strung andintense.Theytypicallyhaveprob lems with isolation, eccentricity, and nihilism. Attheirbest,healthy Fivesarevisionary pioneers,often aheadof their timeand ableto see theworldin anentirelynew way.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: BZ 5The Investigator: Perceptive, innovative, detached

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives feel that they lack inner resources and that too much interaction with others will lead to catastrophic depletion. This Passion leads Fives to withhold themselves from contact with the world. Thus they hold on to their resources and minimize their needs.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Type Five: "It's not okay to be comfortable in the world."

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: 5 Fear of being useless, incapable, or incompetent

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: 5 The desire to be competent (deteriorates into useless specialization)

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Type Five: "Your needs are not a problem."

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Sense of being a detached, outside observer of the world—not part of it. Resists recognizing physical presence and state, feelings and needs.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Type Five responds by retreating from life and reducing their personal needs. Fives believe that they are too frail and insubstantial to safely survive in the world. The only safe place is in their minds, so they stockpile whatever they believe will help them survive until they are ready to rejoin the world.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives also feel that they do not have enough to "bring to the table" to meet the demands of practical life. They retreat until they can learn something or master some skill that would allow them to feel safe enough to come out of hiding.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives are convinced that support is unavailable or not reliable, so they attempt to compensate for the loss of inner guidance by mentally figuring everything out on their own. But because they are "going it alone," they believe they must reduce their need for and attachment to anyone if they are going to break away and be independent.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: The withdrawns (Horney's "moving away from people") include types Four, Five, and Nine. These types do not have much differentiation between their conscious self and their unconscious, unprocessed feelings, thoughts, and impulses.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives might not mind being there, but they would be just as happy at home reading a book or pursuing their own interests. If they stayed, Fives would probably sit on the sidelines and watch everybody else.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: In the Thinking Triad, the Five withdraws for security ("I will be safe if I stay away from others")

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives reject the system and want to work on their own, outside of it. They have little patience with rules or procedures.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Being the expert and having deep information. They focus on the process, objective facts, and maintaining clarity and detachment.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fearing that they are helpless, incapable, and incompetent, Fives make others feel helpless, incompetent, stupid, and incapable.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: 5 That they are never going to find a place in the world or with people

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Detached Fives suddenly become hyperactive and scattered at Seven.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Avaricious, detached Fives become more self-confident and decisive, like healthy Eights.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: BASIC FEAR: Of being helpless, useless, incapable (overwhelmed) BASIC DESIRE: To be capable and competent

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: We have named personality type Five the Investigator because, more than any other type, Fives want to find out why things are the way they are. They want to understand how the world works, whether it is the cosmos, the microscopic world, the animal, vegetable, or mineral kingdom—or the inner world of their imaginations.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Behind Fives' relentless pursuit of knowledge are deep insecurities about their ability to function successfully in the world. Fives feel that they do not have an ability to do things as well as others. But rather than engage directly with activities that might bolster their confidence, Fives "take a step back" into their minds where they feel more capable.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives spend a lot of time observing and contemplating—listening to the sounds of the wind or a synthesizer, or taking notes on the activities in an anthill in their backyard. As they immerse themselves in their observations, they begin to internalize their knowledge and gain a feeling of self-confidence.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Knowledge, understanding, and insight are thus highly valued by Fives, because their identity is built around having ideas and being someone who has something unusual and insightful to say.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Thus, much of their time gets spent collecting and developing ideas and skills they believe will make them feel confident and prepared. It is as if they want to retain everything that they have learned and carry it around in their heads.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Thus, Fives do not expect anything from others, except to be left alone to pursue their own interests unimpeded by anyone else's demands or needs, especially their emotional needs.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives are psychologically stuck in the separation phase of childhood—the period around two to three-and-a-half years old—when children are learning to operate independently of their mothers. For whatever reasons, young Fives felt that the only way to become independent was to make themselves not want nurturing and emotional connection with their mothers.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Learning to cut themselves off from nurturance—even from desiring it—becomes a way of defending themselves against further hurts and frustrations. This becomes significant for adult Fives and explains their reluctance to become more emotionally engaged with others.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: To leave the safety of their minds, to reoccupy the body and feelings, is to reexperience the primal frustration and anguish of their infant selves. Such feelings completely overwhelm a Five's capacity to focus their mind—their basis of self-confidence—and are therefore powerfully defended against.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Self-Preservation Fives are the true loners of the Enneagram, loving solitude and generally avoiding social contact. They feel easily overwhelmed by people, especially in group settings. Although they can be friendly and talkative, they are slow to engage with others and often feel drained by social interactions.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Social Fives like to see themselves as Masters of Wisdom and want to become indispensable through their particular field of expertise (the only person in the office who knows how to fix the computer, for example).

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Sexual Fives like sharing secret information with their intimates. ('I've never told anyone this.') But they are always experiencing some degree of tension between pursuing those they are attracted to and lacking confidence in their social skills.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives let go of the belief that they are separate from the environment—an outside observer—and are thus able to confidently engage in life. They also paradoxically achieve their Basic Desire—to be capable and competent and able to live in the world.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: The Passion of the Five (their 'Capital Sin') is avarice, a particular emotional distortion resulting from their feeling that they are small and incapable of defending themselves in the world. Fear makes Fives shrink inward, and avarice makes them try to hoard whatever minimal resources they have at their disposal.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Average Fives often get locked into what we call preparation mode. They gather more and more information, or endlessly practice, never feeling that they are prepared enough to move into action.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives are the most independent and idiosyncratic of the personality types, the ones who could most appropriately be called loners and even misfits.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Their emotional needs and desires are deeply repressed. Beneath their defenses, this causes Fives pain, of course, but they are able to disconnect from their feelings about their loneliness so they can function.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives, like Nines, have trouble maintaining their sense of self and their own needs when in relationship with others. Unlike Nines, however, Fives attempt to regain their priorities and sense of self by avoiding people.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Average Fives can become absentminded and increasingly detached, not only from people but from their own bodies. They get high-strung and intense and start to ignore their own physical and emotional needs.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Having created an inner world to which they can retreat from the insecurities of their outer life, average Fives tend to become preoccupied with it.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives think a lot about the things that they find the most frightening. They may even make a career out of studying or creating works of art out of things that scare them.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fives attempt to cope with stress by becoming increasingly narrow in their focus and by retreating into the sanctuary of their thoughts.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Underextremestress,Fivesdefendagainsttheiranxietiesby be comingaggressive andinsensitive intheirpursuitof whatevertheyfeelFIVEGOES TO SEVEN [p.237] TYPE FIVE: THE INVESTIGATOR 227 theywantatthetime—like lesshealthySevens.Theymayalsotakeso laceinsubstance abuseofvarious kinds.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Ontheother hand,theymayattempttocutoff allconnection withothers,essentially turning their backson the world in order to further isolatethemselves from"intrusions" sothattheycan followtheirtrainofthoughttoa"log icalconclusion"—usually adarkand self-destructive one.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fivesactualizethemselves andremainhealthybylearningto re- | NT EGRATION: claimandoccupytheirphysicalpresenceand their instinctual energy in the manner of healthyEights.This isbecausethebasisofconfi dence,thefeelingofbeingfull,strong,and capable,arisesfromthein stinctual energyof thebody,notfrommentalstructures.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fivesareextraordinarily observant.and perceptive. They are sensi tiveto theirenvironmentand perceivesubtlechangesordiscrepancies that otherswould likelyoverlook. ManyFivesseemtohaveoneor two oftheirsensesdeveloped toanunusual degree.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fivesdo notlosetheirchildhood curiosity: theykeepaskingques tions,suchas,"Whyisthesky blue?"or"Whydo thingsfalldownand notup?"Fivesdo not takeanythingfor granted—if theywanttoknow whatisundera rock,theygeta spade,digoutthe rock,andtakeagood look.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Becauseoftheir'curiosityandopen-mindedness, healthyFivesare highlyinnovative andinventive.Theabilitytoexploreandplaywithideas canproducevaluable,practical,andoriginalworksanddiscoveries— fromparadigms inscienceormedicine, tostartlingnewachievements in thearts,tofindinganewwaytostoreoldboxesinthegarage.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Fivesenjoysharingtheirfindingswithothers,andtheyoftenserve uptheirobservations oflife'scontradictions withawhimsical senseof humor.They are endlesslyamused—and horrified—by the unfolding strangeness oflife,and theycommunicate thistoothersbychanging thepictureeversoslightlytoexposepreviously invisibleabsurdities.

citations.author: Don Richard Riso & Russ Hudson
citations.quote: Whentheyare presentandgrounded, however, Fivesareabletoknowexactlywhat theyneedtoknow,whentheyneedtoknowit.Theanswertoaques tionarisesnotfromachattering brainbut fromaclearmindthatisat tunedtoreality.
